title: bridge-zephyr: implement standalone LoRa RX processing or remove mode
description: Feature-completeness gap found during Zephyr firmware status audit. The legacy/standalone bridge-zephyr main path supports CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 without CONFIG_LICHEN_L2, but RX processing is explicitly not implemented. That mode can transmit/be configured enough to look real while dropping inbound radio traffic. Evidence: firmware/bridge-zephyr/src/main.c:58-68 describes standalone LoRa mode, and :78 says RX processing not implemented. Acceptance: either implement the standalone RX path through frame parse, SCHC decompression, and IPv6 injection/logging as appropriate, or remove/disable this configuration path so builds use the full CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 integration instead.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
close_reason: Removed the unsupported standalone CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2-without-CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 path from bridge-zephyr so inbound LoRa frames cannot be accepted and dropped outside the full Zephyr L2 ingress pipeline. Added a compile-time guard for that invalid configuration, moved bridge prj.conf to full Zephyr IPv6 networking for LoRa ingress, tightened LICHEN_LORA_L2 Kconfig dependencies/selects, and temporarily wired the AES-CCM helper for LICHEN_LINK until project-LICHEN-9nrr.17 moves that helper out of OSCORE. Tests/builds: west build -b rak4631/nrf52840 firmware/bridge-zephyr -d build/bridge_zephyr_rak4631 --pristine=always passed with CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_L2=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK=y, CONFIG_TINYCRYPT_AES_CCM=y; west build -b nucleo_wl55jc firmware/bridge-zephyr -d build/bridge_zephyr_nucleo_wl55jc --pristine=always passed with CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK unset; git diff --check passed. Review pass 1: found existing lichen_l2 peer API -Wnonnull-compare warnings in the full L2 build and filed project-LICHEN-9nrr.20. Review pass 2: no new defects; AES helper module boundary risk already tracked by project-LICHEN-9nrr.17.
